description:
You are an observability engineer who has just been asked to enable HTTPS on a local-only Grafana instance that lives in the directory  
/home/user/observability.  
For auditing purposes, every change must be accompanied by a concise machine-readable record.

Your goal:  
1. Create a small certificate management sub-structure under /home/user/observability.  
2. Generate a self-signed X.509 certificate for the hostname **grafana-dashboard.local**.  
3. Record several facts about that certificate inside a JSON file that automated tooling can later ingest.  
4. Update the Grafana configuration so that it will actually use the new certificate.  
5. Finally, append one short line to a renewal log so that auditors can confirm what you did.

The entire job should take roughly 5 – 10 shell commands.

Detailed requirements that WILL be checked by the automated test:

A. Directory layout  
   • A new directory called /home/user/observability/certs **must exist** after you finish.  
   • Only files listed below are required inside it; you do not need to restrict additional helper files if you really need them, but the test will look for the exact names given.

B. Certificate and key files  
   • Certificate path: /home/user/observability/certs/grafana-dashboard.local.crt  
   • Private-key path: /home/user/observability/certs/grafana-dashboard.local.key  
   • The certificate MUST be self-signed (Issuer == Subject).  
   • The certificate MUST contain the Common Name (CN) “grafana-dashboard.local”.  
   • Key type: RSA, 2048 bits or higher.  
   • The pair must be readable by your unprivileged user (chmod 600 or 644 is fine).

C. Machine-readable metadata  
   • Create /home/user/observability/certs/cert_info.json  
   • JSON must be **exactly** four top-level keys in the order shown below (ordering matters because the test will compare the raw file content line-by-line):  

     {
       "common_name": "<string>",
       "issuer": "<string>",
       "path": "<string>",
       "sha256_fingerprint": "<string>"
     }

     – "common_name"  must literally be “grafana-dashboard.local”  
     – "issuer"    must literally be “grafana-dashboard.local”  
     – "path"     must literally be “/home/user/observability/certs/grafana-dashboard.local.crt”  
     – "sha256_fingerprint" must be the upper-case colon-separated SHA-256 fingerprint of the certificate (the output of  
       openssl x509 -in … -noout -fingerprint -sha256  
       with the leading “SHA256 Fingerprint=” text removed).  
     The file must end with a single trailing newline.

D. Grafana configuration  
   • There is already a file /home/user/observability/grafana.ini.  
   • Under a [server] section (create it if it does not exist) you must ensure **only** the following four key/value lines appear and are not commented out. Their exact spelling, lowercase letters and spacing, will be tested:  

     [server]  
     protocol = https  
     cert_file = /home/user/observability/certs/grafana-dashboard.local.crt  
     cert_key = /home/user/observability/certs/grafana-dashboard.local.key  

     The test will ignore any other existing sections or keys; it simply has to find these exact four consecutive lines in that order somewhere in the file.

E. Renewal log  
   • Append one line to /home/user/observability/certs/renewal.log using the following fixed format (NO leading or trailing spaces):  

     Renewed: 2024-01-15 | CN=grafana-dashboard.local | Serial=<serialNumberFromCertificate>

     – Use literally the date 2024-01-15.  
     – Replace <serialNumberFromCertificate> with the serial number that actually ended up in your freshly created certificate (hexadecimal, as printed by openssl x509 -in … -noout -serial, but **without** the leading “serial=” text and **without** any colon separators; uppercase A-F).  
   • The renewal.log file may already exist; if it does, your new line must appear at the bottom.

F. General  
   • You do not need root privileges.  
   • Stay inside /home/user/observability and sub-directories—nowhere else.  
   • When you are finished, the three files (crt/key, json, log) and the grafana.ini update must match the exact specifications above so that the automated grader can locate and parse them without ambiguity.

The automated grader will:

1. Confirm that /home/user/observability/certs exists.  
2. Verify that the certificate and key exist, form a valid self-signed pair, and have CN=issuer=grafana-dashboard.local.  
3. Parse cert_info.json and compare every character including JSON key order.  
4. Read grafana.ini and look for the literal 4-line [server] block stated above.  
5. Check that renewal.log ends with exactly one new line in the format specified and that the serial number matches the certificate.

Accomplish all of the above through direct shell interaction. Feel free to display any console output you find useful while working.
truth:
Expected artefacts and properties:

1. Directory that must exist: /home/user/observability/certs

2. Files that must exist after agent completion
   2.1 /home/user/observability/certs/grafana-dashboard.local.crt
       • X.509, self-signed
       • Subject  : CN = grafana-dashboard.local
       • Issuer   : CN = grafana-dashboard.local
       • RSA key ≥ 2048 bits
   2.2 /home/user/observability/certs/grafana-dashboard.local.key
       • PEM, matching private key
   2.3 /home/user/observability/certs/cert_info.json
       • Exact 4-line JSON text (including newlines):
{
  "common_name": "grafana-dashboard.local",
  "issuer": "grafana-dashboard.local",
  "path": "/home/user/observability/certs/grafana-dashboard.local.crt",
  "sha256_fingerprint": "<AUTO_FILLED>"
}
         where <AUTO_FILLED> equals the SHA-256 fingerprint of file in 2.1 with colons and uppercase, ex:
         15:4F:42:…  (exact value unpredictable)
   2.4 /home/user/observability/certs/renewal.log
       • Last line must be:
         Renewed: 2024-01-15 | CN=grafana-dashboard.local | Serial=<SERIAL>
         where <SERIAL> is the certificate serial number, uppercase hexadecimal, no prefix.

3. grafana.ini
   • Must contain the literal consecutive block:
[server]
protocol = https
cert_file = /home/user/observability/certs/grafana-dashboard.local.crt
cert_key = /home/user/observability/certs/grafana-dashboard.local.key

4. The grader will compute fingerprint and serial number directly from the generated certificate, then compare with cert_info.json and renewal.log respectively.

No privileged access needed; all resides under /home/user/observability.
